theory_page_sentence_transformations_rel.name: Sentence Transformations — Relative Clauses / Linking Words

sentence_transformations_relative_clause.name: sentence transformations relative clauses linking words all levels

questions.question: Combine with who: This is the teacher. She helped me. This is the teacher {a1}.
Combine with that: I bought a phone. It works offline. I bought a phone {a1}.
Add a non-defining clause: My sister is a doctor. She lives in Lviv. My sister, {a1}, lives in Lviv.
Omit the relative pronoun where possible: The book that I borrowed was useful. The book {a1} was useful.
Reduce the clause: The man who is standing near the door is my uncle. The man {a1} is my uncle.
Rewrite with because: It was cold, so we stayed inside. We stayed inside {a1}.
Rewrite with although: It rained, but we went out. {a1}, we went out.
Rewrite with so: The road was icy. We drove slowly. The road was icy, {a1}.
Rewrite with to: I called him because I wanted to ask a question. I called him {a1}.
Choose the link that preserves the relation: She was tired, but she finished the work. {a1}, she finished the work.
Avoid the mistake: This is the house {a1}.
Combine with whose: I met a girl. Her brother is a pilot. I met a girl {a1}.

options: whose brother he is a pilot
